commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the audience contract plus final CI/security completion.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not change dependencies, workflows, secrets, or external execution, and it preserves owner-tool downgrade as structured metadata. (sha=0bc174ab03049efd7b392d9294e5067fb7d3fa25)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `SystemEvent.audience`, wraps internal-audience system events as hidden runtime context, and migrates isolated cron-awareness events plus related heartbeat and collect-mode transcript paths.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `main` has no audience field, queues cron awareness through `enqueueSystemEvent`, and drains selected generic events as visible `System:` lines. I did not run a live cron/channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the audience contract plus final CI/security completion.

target.title: feat(events): add audience field for hidden runtime-context system events
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses part of #69492 (system-event-shape consumer-path leakage; under umbrella #69208 Track B).

Adds an optional `audience: 'internal' | 'user-facing'` field to `SystemEvent`, defaulting to `'user-facing'` (full backward-compat for every existing caller). Internal-audience events are wrapped at the consumer integration point in the existing `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` delimiters with the canonical header lines from `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`. The agent runtime sees the content as runtime context; user-facing surfaces strip it via the already-installed `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers (`sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`, `memory-host-sdk/host/session-files.ts`, `agents/internal-events.ts`, history readers, Control UI extract).

Mirrors the producer-side runtime-context delimiter pattern landed in:
- `e918e5f75c` (#71761) ŌĆö agent runtime-context custom entry
- `6e985a421d` ŌĆö webchat runtime-context

No new persistence shape, no consumer-side filter (avoids the audit-bypass concern flagged on #69217), no display-layer per-surface patch (avoids the rejection pattern from #69366).

## Why this scope is tight

This primitive is the **hidden runtime-context lane only**. It is not a delivery-routing primitive. Events with a positive user delivery contract (exec completion via `notifyOnExit`, cron payloads, heartbeat acks) are NOT migrated ŌĆö those have heartbeat-driven explicit-relay paths (`buildExecEventPrompt` / `buildCronEventPrompt`) plus tactical producer-side skips like `bd60df3e53` (`fix: silence cron exec completion noise`). Marking them internal would suppress delivery on regular reply turns where the model is told to keep wrapped content private.

The user-deliverable noise question stays open; the maintainer's recent pattern is case-by-case (`bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`), and that lane should remain.

## Changes

- `src/infra/system-events.ts` ŌĆö add `SystemEventAudience` type and optional `audience` field on `SystemEvent` + `SystemEventOptions`. Default `"user-facing"`. JSDoc explicitly documents the scope (hidden runtime-context, not delivery routing) and the operator-surface caveat (`peekSystemEvents`/`peekSystemEventEntries` keep showing internal events for debugging). `areSystemEventsEqual` honors the field so duplicate-suppression and `consumeSystemEventEntries` prefix-match keep working.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session-system-events.ts` ŌĆö partition the drain output into user-facing lines and internal lines. User-facing lines emit as plain `System: ...` (unchanged). Internal lines are wrapped in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` with the canonical header (matching `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`). `escapeInternalRuntimeContextDelimiters` handles adversarial inner tokens.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` ŌĆö migrate `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` to `audience: "internal"`. This event is queued only after the isolated agent run has already delivered to the user via its own channel (gated on `delivered === true` and `shouldQueueCronAwareness`); the main session needs hidden context, not a fake-user `System (untrusted): ...` bubble.

## Explicitly NOT migrated

- `maybeNotifyOnExit` (exec completion) ŌĆö `notifyOnExit` delivery contract.
- `executeMainSessionCronJob` (cron payload) ŌĆö user-scheduled reminder content.
- Cron auto-disable warning, cron failure alert ŌĆö user-relevant.
- Cron `wake()` API ŌĆö exposes arbitrary user-supplied text via gateway `cron.wake`.
- Gateway presence (`server-methods/system.ts`), node events (`server-node-events.ts`).
- Task registry, session-maintenance-warning, plugin runtime ŌĆö caller-dependent / operator-facing / third-party.

JSDoc on `SystemEventAudience` explicitly excludes these from the lane and points at the existing delivery paths.

## Follow-up plan (out of scope for this PR)

This PR is intentionally one-step. It introduces the primitive and migrates exactly one producer (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) so the wrap-on-drain integration point can be reviewed against a real call site. No additional audience-PR is pre-committed; future migrations should be evaluated case-by-case as concrete leakage reports surface.

**Candidates that may earn `audience: "internal"` later if a real need is demonstrated:** debug- or awareness-style producers that are queued *after* the user has already received delivery on a different channel (the same shape as `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` here). Each would need its own justification ŌĆö no batch migration.

**Explicitly NOT in any audience-PR sequence** (these stay in the maintainer's case-by-case tactical-patch lane, e.g. `bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`):
- exec completion (`maybeNotifyOnExit`)
- cron payload delivery (`executeMainSessionCronJob`)
- heartbeat acks
- any other producer with a positive user delivery contract

A larger abstraction was floated during peer review ŌĆö a 3-state shape (`prompt-visible` | `hidden-context` | `dedicated-delivery`) for events that have both an internal-awareness *and* a user-delivery contract. **Not on the roadmap.** That redesign collides with the heartbeat-runner explicit-relay paths and the producer-side skips the maintainer has been adding directly; pursuing it would re-open the rejection patterns from #69366. Flagging here only so future readers know it was considered and deliberately not pursued.

If maintainer direction differs, happy to follow guidance on #69492 before landing further migrations.


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron-awareness system events (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`, queued onto the main session **after** the isolated cron has already delivered the result on its own channel) were appearing in the main session as `System (untrusted): cron run ŌĆ”` user-visible bubbles, even though their purpose is internal awareness for the agent rather than a user-deliverable message. The fix migrates that producer to `audience: "internal"` so the autoreply integration site wraps the event in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` ŌĆ” `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` and the existing `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers remove it from every user-facing surface.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw gateway daemon on macOS Sonoma (Darwin 23.6.0), Node 25.6.0, running multiple isolated cron jobs against an 11-account Matrix deployment. Patched dist (built from the 2-commit local branch on top of `mercury/local-patches`) has been running continuously since reload.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built patched dist with `pnpm openclaw build`'s equivalent (`pnpm build`), reloaded the gateway LaunchDaemon (`sudo launchctl bootout system/ai.openclaw.gateway && sudo launchctl bootstrap system /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`), and let scheduled isolated cron jobs (`~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`) execute on their normal schedules (`mercury` agent, gpt-5-mini / claude-sonnet-4-6 backends, Matrix delivery target).
- Evidence after fix:
  - **The wrap delimiters are already a proven primitive in the running gateway** ŌĆö this PR adds a new producer to that lane. Live transcript excerpt from `~/.openclaw/agents/devin/sessions/3998fa7e-ŌĆ”03d.jsonl` showing the `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `OpenClaw runtime context (internal):` block landing in the user-message context (auto-stripped from user-visible surfaces by `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) ŌĆö terminal output below:
    ```
    [Sun 2026-05-03 21:14 EDT] <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    [Internal task completion event]
    source: subagent
    session_key: agent:devin:subagent:9d4f2234-ŌĆ”
    ```
  - **Cron isolated runs completing successfully on the patched daemon** ŌĆö redacted runtime log excerpt from `~/.openclaw/cron/runs/4309f6e7-f09d-4011-93ff-21726e3aa76d.jsonl` (the actual openclaw cron run record):
    ```json
    {"action":"finished","status":"ok","summary":"Top hit: openclaw-docs/automation/cron-jobs ŌĆ” [mercury | claude-sonnet-4-6]","sessionKey":"agent:mercury:cron:4309f6e7-ŌĆ”","durationMs":27268,"model":"gpt-5-mini","provider":"openai"}
    ```
    These are the exact runs whose post-delivery `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` calls produce the events this PR migrates to `audience: "internal"`. Neither the cron run itself nor the main session has thrown since the rebased dist was deployed.
  - Gateway uptime: the `openclaw gateway` daemon has been continuously up since the patched-dist reload, processing real ws traffic ŌĆö `agents.list`, `chat.history`, `sessions.list` round-trips all 400-500ms in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log`; `[sessions/store] pruned stale session entries` (an adjacent local patch) firing on schedule.
  - **Fresh post-rebase end-to-end harness run against HEAD `0bc174ab03`** (refreshed 2026-05-20 after rebasing onto current `upstream/main` to absorb the `currentTurnKind` ŌåÆ `currentInboundEventKind` rename and the rest of the 1000+ adjacent commits since 2fcaab0010; the earlier `c76b3c19ec` proof is preserved in the gateway-runtime evidence above): `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` drives the actual production code paths (`enqueueSystemEvent` ŌåÆ `drainFormattedSystemEventBlock` ŌåÆ `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) on a throwaway sessionKey, with no mocks. Command: `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-pr72201.ts`. Live terminal output:
    ```
    === (A) post-enqueue queue snapshot ===
    [
      {
        "audience": "internal",
        "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
        "contextKey": "cron:proof-pr72201",
        "text": "Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only."
      }
    ]

    === (B) rendered prompt body (model-visible) ===
    <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    System: [2026-05-20T19:07:04Z] Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only.
    <<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>

    --- block.forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    --- wrap checks ---
      BEGIN delimiter present: true
      END delimiter present  : true
      END after BEGIN        : true
      canonical header line  : true
      event body present     : true
      forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    === (C) user-facing view (after stripInternalRuntimeContext) ===
    ""

    --- strip checks ---
      no BEGIN in user view  : true
      no event body leaked   : true

    === RESULT ===
    PASS
    ```
    All three claims verified end-to-end on the rebased HEAD: (A) the event is queued with `audience: "internal"` and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` (operator-side `peekSystemEventEntries` exposes it for debugging); (B) the prompt body the model sees is wrapped in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` ŌĆ” `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` with the canonical header lines and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` flowing back on the returned `FormattedSystemEventBlock`; (C) `stripInternalRuntimeContext` reduces the user-facing view to an empty string ŌĆö nothing from the internal event leaks to the transcript / history reader / channel surface. The harness lives at `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (local artifact, not pushed in this PR ŌĆö output above is the captured terminal log).
- Observed result after fix: cron-awareness events queued after isolated cron delivery no longer reach user-facing chat surfaces. The isolated cron continues to deliver its own result on its configured channel (unchanged), and the main session sees the awareness event only as wrapped runtime context for agent reasoning ŌĆö not as a fake `System (untrusted)` user bubble. Live runtime logs in `gateway.log` show no new error or stack trace lines from this code path on the patched daemon.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a side-by-side pre-patch vs post-patch screenshot of a Matrix client showing the absence of the `System (untrusted): cron run ŌĆ”` bubble ŌĆö the negative-evidence form is hard to capture cleanly because the bubble simply stops appearing. The unit test in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` asserts the `audience: "internal"` is set at the producer site as the structural regression. The broader audience-routing primitive (additional producers beyond `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) was intentionally **not** migrated in this PR; that scope is deferred to follow-up case-by-case work per #69492.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- src/infra/system-events.test.ts` passes (34/34, +9 audience tests covering default, round-trip, equality, wrap shape, ordering, only-user-facing, only-internal, escape, end-to-end strip).
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` passes (47/47); existing assertion extended with `audience: "internal"`.
- [x] `pnpm build` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm check` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passes (per `feedback_openclaw_check_gaps.md`-style validation gap closure).
- [x] `codex review --base upstream/main` clean ("No discrete correctness issues were identified in the diff.").

## Pre-PR peer review

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation. The peer review caught a contract-confusion issue between two earlier drafts (selective-drain vs wrap-on-drain) and pushed for the wrap-on-drain design landed here. Round-2 verdict: approve with PR-body wording tweak (avoid GitHub auto-close keywords for a partial fix; "Addresses part of #69492" is the framing applied here).

## Degree of testing

Lightly tested. Unit tests cover the primitive default, round-trip, equality, the wrap shape, ordering, edge cases, end-to-end strip via `stripInternalRuntimeContext`, and adversarial delimiter-token escaping. The single migrated producer has a regression assertion in the existing test. No e2e or live-channel coverage added.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- [x] `pnpm test -- src/infra/system-events.test.ts` passes (34/34, +9 audience tests covering default, round-trip, equality, wrap shape, ordering, only-user-facing, only-internal, escape, end-to-end strip).
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` passes (47/47); existing assertion extended with `audience: "internal"`.
- [x] `pnpm build` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm check` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passes (per `feedback_openclaw_check_gaps.md`-style validation gap closure).
- [x] `codex review --base upstream/main` clean ("No discrete correctness issues were identified in the diff.").

## Pre-PR peer review

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation. The peer review caught a contract-confusion issue between two earlier drafts (selective-drain vs wrap-on-drain) and pushed for the wrap-on-drain design landed here. Round-2 verdict: approve with PR-body wording tweak (avoid GitHub auto-close keywords for a partial fix; "Addresses part of #69492" is the framing applied here).

## Degree of testing

Lightly tested. Unit tests cover the primitive default, round-trip, equality, the wrap shape, ordering, edge cases, end-to-end strip via `stripInternalRuntimeContext`, and adversarial delimiter-token escaping. The single migrated producer has a regression assertion in the existing test. No e2e or live-channel coverage added.

target.title: feat(events): add audience field for hidden runtime-context system events
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses part of #69492 (system-event-shape consumer-path leakage; under umbrella #69208 Track B).

Adds an optional `audience: 'internal' | 'user-facing'` field to `SystemEvent`, defaulting to `'user-facing'` (full backward-compat for every existing caller). Internal-audience events are wrapped at the consumer integration point in the existing `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` delimiters with the canonical header lines from `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`. The agent runtime sees the content as runtime context; user-facing surfaces strip it via the already-installed `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers (`sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`, `memory-host-sdk/host/session-files.ts`, `agents/internal-events.ts`, history readers, Control UI extract).

Mirrors the producer-side runtime-context delimiter pattern landed in:
- `e918e5f75c` (#71761) ŌĆö agent runtime-context custom entry
- `6e985a421d` ŌĆö webchat runtime-context

No new persistence shape, no consumer-side filter (avoids the audit-bypass concern flagged on #69217), no display-layer per-surface patch (avoids the rejection pattern from #69366).

## Why this scope is tight

This primitive is the **hidden runtime-context lane only**. It is not a delivery-routing primitive. Events with a positive user delivery contract (exec completion via `notifyOnExit`, cron payloads, heartbeat acks) are NOT migrated ŌĆö those have heartbeat-driven explicit-relay paths (`buildExecEventPrompt` / `buildCronEventPrompt`) plus tactical producer-side skips like `bd60df3e53` (`fix: silence cron exec completion noise`). Marking them internal would suppress delivery on regular reply turns where the model is told to keep wrapped content private.

The user-deliverable noise question stays open; the maintainer's recent pattern is case-by-case (`bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`), and that lane should remain.

## Changes

- `src/infra/system-events.ts` ŌĆö add `SystemEventAudience` type and optional `audience` field on `SystemEvent` + `SystemEventOptions`. Default `"user-facing"`. JSDoc explicitly documents the scope (hidden runtime-context, not delivery routing) and the operator-surface caveat (`peekSystemEvents`/`peekSystemEventEntries` keep showing internal events for debugging). `areSystemEventsEqual` honors the field so duplicate-suppression and `consumeSystemEventEntries` prefix-match keep working.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session-system-events.ts` ŌĆö partition the drain output into user-facing lines and internal lines. User-facing lines emit as plain `System: ...` (unchanged). Internal lines are wrapped in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` with the canonical header (matching `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`). `escapeInternalRuntimeContextDelimiters` handles adversarial inner tokens.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` ŌĆö migrate `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` to `audience: "internal"`. This event is queued only after the isolated agent run has already delivered to the user via its own channel (gated on `delivered === true` and `shouldQueueCronAwareness`); the main session needs hidden context, not a fake-user `System (untrusted): ...` bubble.

## Explicitly NOT migrated

- `maybeNotifyOnExit` (exec completion) ŌĆö `notifyOnExit` delivery contract.
- `executeMainSessionCronJob` (cron payload) ŌĆö user-scheduled reminder content.
- Cron auto-disable warning, cron failure alert ŌĆö user-relevant.
- Cron `wake()` API ŌĆö exposes arbitrary user-supplied text via gateway `cron.wake`.
- Gateway presence (`server-methods/system.ts`), node events (`server-node-events.ts`).
- Task registry, session-maintenance-warning, plugin runtime ŌĆö caller-dependent / operator-facing / third-party.

JSDoc on `SystemEventAudience` explicitly excludes these from the lane and points at the existing delivery paths.

## Follow-up plan (out of scope for this PR)

This PR is intentionally one-step. It introduces the primitive and migrates exactly one producer (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) so the wrap-on-drain integration point can be reviewed against a real call site. No additional audience-PR is pre-committed; future migrations should be evaluated case-by-case as concrete leakage reports surface.

**Candidates that may earn `audience: "internal"` later if a real need is demonstrated:** debug- or awareness-style producers that are queued *after* the user has already received delivery on a different channel (the same shape as `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` here). Each would need its own justification ŌĆö no batch migration.

**Explicitly NOT in any audience-PR sequence** (these stay in the maintainer's case-by-case tactical-patch lane, e.g. `bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`):
- exec completion (`maybeNotifyOnExit`)
- cron payload delivery (`executeMainSessionCronJob`)
- heartbeat acks
- any other producer with a positive user delivery contract

A larger abstraction was floated during peer review ŌĆö a 3-state shape (`prompt-visible` | `hidden-context` | `dedicated-delivery`) for events that have both an internal-awareness *and* a user-delivery contract. **Not on the roadmap.** That redesign collides with the heartbeat-runner explicit-relay paths and the producer-side skips the maintainer has been adding directly; pursuing it would re-open the rejection patterns from #69366. Flagging here only so future readers know it was considered and deliberately not pursued.

If maintainer direction differs, happy to follow guidance on #69492 before landing further migrations.


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron-awareness system events (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`, queued onto the main session **after** the isolated cron has already delivered the result on its own channel) were appearing in the main session as `System (untrusted): cron run ŌĆ”` user-visible bubbles, even though their purpose is internal awareness for the agent rather than a user-deliverable message. The fix migrates that producer to `audience: "internal"` so the autoreply integration site wraps the event in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` ŌĆ” `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` and the existing `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers remove it from every user-facing surface.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw gateway daemon on macOS Sonoma (Darwin 23.6.0), Node 25.6.0, running multiple isolated cron jobs against an 11-account Matrix deployment. Patched dist (built from the 2-commit local branch on top of `mercury/local-patches`) has been running continuously since reload.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built patched dist with `pnpm openclaw build`'s equivalent (`pnpm build`), reloaded the gateway LaunchDaemon (`sudo launchctl bootout system/ai.openclaw.gateway && sudo launchctl bootstrap system /Library/LaunchDaemons/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`), and let scheduled isolated cron jobs (`~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`) execute on their normal schedules (`mercury` agent, gpt-5-mini / claude-sonnet-4-6 backends, Matrix delivery target).
- Evidence after fix:
  - **The wrap delimiters are already a proven primitive in the running gateway** ŌĆö this PR adds a new producer to that lane. Live transcript excerpt from `~/.openclaw/agents/devin/sessions/3998fa7e-ŌĆ”03d.jsonl` showing the `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `OpenClaw runtime context (internal):` block landing in the user-message context (auto-stripped from user-visible surfaces by `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) ŌĆö terminal output below:
    ```
    [Sun 2026-05-03 21:14 EDT] <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    [Internal task completion event]
    source: subagent
    session_key: agent:devin:subagent:9d4f2234-ŌĆ”
    ```
  - **Cron isolated runs completing successfully on the patched daemon** ŌĆö redacted runtime log excerpt from `~/.openclaw/cron/runs/4309f6e7-f09d-4011-93ff-21726e3aa76d.jsonl` (the actual openclaw cron run record):
    ```json
    {"action":"finished","status":"ok","summary":"Top hit: openclaw-docs/automation/cron-jobs ŌĆ” [mercury | claude-sonnet-4-6]","sessionKey":"agent:mercury:cron:4309f6e7-ŌĆ”","durationMs":27268,"model":"gpt-5-mini","provider":"openai"}
    ```
    These are the exact runs whose post-delivery `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` calls produce the events this PR migrates to `audience: "internal"`. Neither the cron run itself nor the main session has thrown since the rebased dist was deployed.
  - Gateway uptime: the `openclaw gateway` daemon has been continuously up since the patched-dist reload, processing real ws traffic ŌĆö `agents.list`, `chat.history`, `sessions.list` round-trips all 400-500ms in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log`; `[sessions/store] pruned stale session entries` (an adjacent local patch) firing on schedule.
  - **Fresh post-rebase end-to-end harness run against HEAD `0bc174ab03`** (refreshed 2026-05-20 after rebasing onto current `upstream/main` to absorb the `currentTurnKind` ŌåÆ `currentInboundEventKind` rename and the rest of the 1000+ adjacent commits since 2fcaab0010; the earlier `c76b3c19ec` proof is preserved in the gateway-runtime evidence above): `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` drives the actual production code paths (`enqueueSystemEvent` ŌåÆ `drainFormattedSystemEventBlock` ŌåÆ `stripInternalRuntimeContext`) on a throwaway sessionKey, with no mocks. Command: `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-pr72201.ts`. Live terminal output:
    ```
    === (A) post-enqueue queue snapshot ===
    [
      {
        "audience": "internal",
        "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
        "contextKey": "cron:proof-pr72201",
        "text": "Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only."
      }
    ]

    === (B) rendered prompt body (model-visible) ===
    <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    System: [2026-05-20T19:07:04Z] Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only.
    <<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>

    --- block.forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    --- wrap checks ---
      BEGIN delimiter present: true
      END delimiter present  : true
      END after BEGIN        : true
      canonical header line  : true
      event body present     : true
      forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true

    === (C) user-facing view (after stripInternalRuntimeContext) ===
    ""

    --- strip checks ---
      no BEGIN in user view  : true
      no event body leaked   : true

    === RESULT ===
    PASS
    ```
    All three claims verified end-to-end on the rebased HEAD: (A) the event is queued with `audience: "internal"` and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` (operator-side `peekSystemEventEntries` exposes it for debugging); (B) the prompt body the model sees is wrapped in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` ŌĆ” `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` with the canonical header lines and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` flowing back on the returned `FormattedSystemEventBlock`; (C) `stripInternalRuntimeContext` reduces the user-facing view to an empty string ŌĆö nothing from the internal event leaks to the transcript / history reader / channel surface. The harness lives at `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (local artifact, not pushed in this PR ŌĆö output above is the captured terminal log).
- Observed result after fix: cron-awareness events queued after isolated cron delivery no longer reach user-facing chat surfaces. The isolated cron continues to deliver its own result on its configured channel (unchanged), and the main session sees the awareness event only as wrapped runtime context for agent reasoning ŌĆö not as a fake `System (untrusted)` user bubble. Live runtime logs in `gateway.log` show no new error or stack trace lines from this code path on the patched daemon.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a side-by-side pre-patch vs post-patch screenshot of a Matrix client showing the absence of the `System (untrusted): cron run ŌĆ”` bubble ŌĆö the negative-evidence form is hard to capture cleanly because the bubble simply stops appearing. The unit test in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` asserts the `audience: "internal"` is set at the producer site as the structural regression. The broader audience-routing primitive (additional producers beyond `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) was intentionally **not** migrated in this PR; that scope is deferred to follow-up case-by-case work per #69492.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- src/infra/system-events.test.ts` passes (34/34, +9 audience tests covering default, round-trip, equality, wrap shape, ordering, only-user-facing, only-internal, escape, end-to-end strip).
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` passes (47/47); existing assertion extended with `audience: "internal"`.
- [x] `pnpm build` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm check` passes locally.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passes (per `feedback_openclaw_check_gaps.md`-style validation gap closure).
- [x] `codex review --base upstream/main` clean ("No discrete correctness issues were identified in the diff.").

## Pre-PR peer review

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation. The peer review caught a contract-confusion issue between two earlier drafts (selective-drain vs wrap-on-drain) and pushed for the wrap-on-drain design landed here. Round-2 verdict: approve with PR-body wording tweak (avoid GitHub auto-close keywords for a partial fix; "Addresses part of #69492" is the framing applied here).

## Degree of testing

Lightly tested. Unit tests cover the primitive default, round-trip, equality, the wrap shape, ordering, edge cases, end-to-end strip via `stripInternalRuntimeContext`, and adversarial delimiter-token escaping. The single migrated producer has a regression assertion in the existing test. No e2e or live-channel coverage added.

    All three claims verified end-to-end on the rebased HEAD: (A) the event is queued with `audience: "internal"` and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` (operator-side `peekSystemEventEntries` exposes it for debugging); (B) the prompt body the model sees is wrapped in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` ŌĆ” `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` with the canonical header lines and `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse: true` flowing back on the returned `FormattedSystemEventBlock`; (C) `stripInternalRuntimeContext` reduces the user-facing view to an empty string ŌĆö nothing from the internal event leaks to the transcript / history reader / channel surface. The harness lives at `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (local artifact, not pushed in this PR ŌĆö output above is the captured terminal log).
- Observed result after fix: cron-awareness events queued after isolated cron delivery no longer reach user-facing chat surfaces. The isolated cron continues to deliver its own result on its configured channel (unchanged), and the main session sees the awareness event only as wrapped runtime context for agent reasoning ŌĆö not as a fake `System (untrusted)` user bubble. Live runtime logs in `gateway.log` show no new error or stack trace lines from this code path on the patched daemon.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a side-by-side pre-patch vs post-patch screenshot of a Matrix client showing the absence of the `System (untrusted): cron run ŌĆ”` bubble ŌĆö the negative-evidence form is hard to capture cleanly because the bubble simply stops appearing. The unit test in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` asserts the `audience: "internal"` is set at the producer site as the structural regression. The broader audience-routing primitive (additional producers beyond `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) was intentionally **not** migrated in this PR; that scope is deferred to follow-up case-by-case work per #69492.

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation.
